commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The blocker is a security-sensitive availability repair in an external PR; a contributor or maintainer should revise the patch and rerun focused proof before merge.; Needs attention: The security hardening is incomplete because token responses can still be buffered without a size cap before the bounded reader runs.; Review finding: [P1] Move the cap before the response clone — extensions/openai/openai-chatgpt... commands.review_summary:
The PR imports shared provider HTTP bounded readers, uses them for OpenAI Codex OAuth token exchange/refresh success and error bodies, and adds OAuth-flow tests for oversized and malformed responses.

PR surface: Source +15, Tests +85. Total +100 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: the token response is consumed with `response.arrayBuffer()` before the new bounded readers can run, so a streaming oversized response can still be buffered first. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The blocker is a security-sensitive availability repair in an external PR; a contributor or maintainer should revise the patch and rerun focused proof before merge.

target.title: fix(openai): bound Codex OAuth JSON and error response reads to prevent OOM
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`exchangeAuthorizationCode` and `refreshAccessToken` in the OpenAI Codex OAuth
flow call `response.json()` and `response.text()` without size limits on the
token endpoint response. A hostile or buggy endpoint could stream an unbounded
JSON body or error body, exhausting gateway memory.

## Why This Change Was Made

The plugin SDK provides `readProviderJsonResponse` (16 MiB cap with JSON
parsing) and `readResponseTextLimited` (16 MiB capped text read) for this
exact pattern. Other OAuth flows (xAI OAuth, Google OAuth) already use them
for the same purpose.

## User Impact

No user-facing behavior change under normal operation. Oversized or malformed
token responses now safely return a `{ type: "failed" }` result instead of
potentially exhausting memory.

## Evidence

- `pnpm test extensions/openai/openai-chatgpt-oauth-flow.runtime.test.ts` — 14
  tests passed (10 existing + 4 new bounding tests)
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

## After-fix Proof

Real loopback HTTP server proof on this PR branch:

```bash
node --import tsx proof-openai-oauth-bound.mts
```

```
=== Test 1: readProviderJsonResponse caps oversized streaming body ===
  chunks sent: 17
  error: openai-codex-token-exchange: JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes
  PASS

=== Test 2: readProviderJsonResponse with valid JSON ===
  parsed access_token: tok_abc
  PASS

=== Test 3: readResponseTextLimited handles error body ===
  error text: "error detail: invalid_grant"
  PASS

=== Test 4: readProviderJsonResponse with malformed JSON ===
  error: openai-codex-token-exchange: malformed JSON response
  PASS

PASS: all OpenAI Codex OAuth bounding paths verified
```

**What was not tested**: Live OpenAI Codex OAuth token endpoint.

## Tests

`extensions/openai/openai-chatgpt-oauth-flow.runtime.test.ts` — 4 new tests:
- oversized token exchange JSON response
- malformed token exchange JSON response
- capped error body on failed token exchange
- oversized token refresh JSON response
